records.query: What is polyurea used for in protective coatings?
records.answer: Polyurea is often used in protective coatings for applications where enhanced properties such as crack bridging, toughness, corrosion or abrasion resistance are required. Common uses include pipes and tanks for wastewater infrastructure, roofing protection, bridge deck waterproofing, and truck bedliners for both personal and industrial use [5.10, p. 467-48].

Thermoplastic elastomers (TPEs) are materials that can be processed in the same manner as conventional thermoplastics but offer performance and properties similar to those of thermoset rubbers. Specifically, a thermoplastic vulcanizate (TPV), which is also referred to as an elastomeric alloy in some earlier literature, is a generic class of TPEs with a chemically cross-linked rubber phase dispersed within a continuous matrix of thermoplastic material [Coatings Materials and Surface Coatings (Tracton, 2007), Chapter 10, p. 1-2].

records.query: ¿Qué función cumple un primer en un sistema de recubrimiento?
records.answer: Un primer en un sistema de recubrimiento generalmente se refiere a un preparador de superficie o imprimación, que sirve para mejorar las propiedades de adherencia y porosidad del material base, permitiendo que el recubrimiento se aplique de manera más efectiva. Sin embargo, no hay información específica en losexcerptos proporcionados sobre la función exacta de un "primer". Los textos mencionan preparación de superficie y imprimaciones pero no se detallan roles específicos para una fase denominada “primer” [Libro Pinturas_y_recubrimientos_Giúdice, Unknown, p. 110].

records.query: What is a zinc-rich coating used for?
records.answer: Zinc-rich coatings are used to provide excellent galvanic protection in aggressive environments. They are particularly useful for protecting ship hull superstructures, marine structures, highway bridges, chemical plant equipment, and other installations exposed to high humidity and salt [Book, Chapter, p. 138]. Additionally, they can be used to repair galvanized or inorganic zinc-rich coatings [Book, Chapter, p. 137].

El texto proporcionado no contiene información específica sobre lo que es la corrosión por picadura. Los pasajes hablan sobre la corrosión general, el mecanismo de la corrosión electroquímica y la preparación de superficies con hidrochorreo, pero no mencionan la corrosión por picadura [Libro Pinturas_y_recubrimientos_Giúdice, II.14 RESINAS DE SILICONA, p. 125].

Cathodic Protection is an electrochemical process used to protect metals from corrosion. It works by passing a direct electrical current to the metal surface, which can be achieved through either galvanic or impressed current systems [Cathodic_Protection, Unknown, p. 1]. This method helps in arresting corrosion on structures like above-ground storage tanks that are in contact with sand pads or soil [Cathodic_Protection, Unknown, p. 1].

La corrosión galvánica es un tipo de corrosión acelerada que ocurre cuando se produce un contacto eléctrico entre dos metales o conductores no metálicos en un medio conductor, también conocido como electrólito [GLOSSARY, G, p. 12]. Este fenómeno se caracteriza porque el metal menos noble (el anodo) actúa como fuente de electrones, mientras que el metal más noble (el cátodo) es corrosivo debido a su reacción con el medio conductor [Deposition Technologies for Films and Coatings, Chapter 14, p. 766-767].

Stress corrosion cracking (SCC) develops when a tough polymer is stressed for an extended period under loads that are small relative to the polymer’s yield point [Libro Corrosion of Linings and Coatings - Schweitzer, Chapter 6. Sheet Linings, p. 309-311]. The higher the molecular weight of the polymer, other things being equal, the less likelihood there is of environmental stress cracking since longer chain polymers tend to crystallize less and have greater load-bearing capacity [ibid].

SCC occurs from the combined influence of tensile stress and a corrosive medium [Handbook of Corrosion Engineering, 5. Corrosion Failures, p. 355-357]. The presence of trace impurities such as hydrogen, nitrogen, or oxygen can affect the susceptibility to SCC in specific molten metals [ibid].

Zirconium alloys are susceptible to stress-corrosion cracking (SCC) under certain conditions, particularly in concentrated methanol solutions containing heavy metal chlorides, gaseous iodine, or fused salts with iodine, even though zirconium and its alloys are generally resistant to SCC in seawater and most aqueous chemical media [Handbook of Corrosion Engineering, 8. Materials Selection, p. 785-786].

Los inhibidores de corrosión son sustancias químicas que, cuando se añaden en concentraciones pequeñas a un medio ambiente, efectivamente reducen la velocidad del proceso de corrosión [Libro Pinturas_y_recubrimientos_Giúdice, Unknown, p. 213-214].
